title: Adas Polo (Persian Lentil Rice with Dates and Raisins)

ingredients.name: brown or green lentils
visual_checks: whole masoor or green moong; rinse well
ingredients.notes: whole masoor or green moong; rinse well

subs.name: red lentils (split masoor) — cook for only 8 minutes as they soften faster
subs.notes: texture will be softer and less distinct

subs.name: dried figs or chopped dried apricots
dried cranberries for a tart note

steps.text: Rinse and simmer the lentils in 400 ml water on medium flame for 15–18 minutes until just tender but still holding their shape — do not overcook. Drain and season lightly with salt.
In a separate pot, boil 700 ml salted water. Drain the soaked rice and cook in the boiling water for 6 minutes until half-cooked — the grains should be firm at the centre. Drain through a sieve.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a heavy pot on medium. Add the sliced onion and cook for 8 minutes until golden and beginning to caramelise. Add cumin, cinnamon and turmeric, stir for 30 seconds.
Add the cooked lentils, dates and raisins to the onion. Mix gently. Layer the parboiled rice over the lentil mixture in the same pot, mounding it in the centre.
Drizzle 1 tablespoon oil over the rice. Cover with a cloth-wrapped lid and cook on the lowest flame for 20–25 minutes until the rice is perfectly fluffy and a golden crust forms on the base.
To serve, scoop the rice and lentil layer onto a platter and place the tahdig (crispy crust) on the side or on top. The dish is traditionally eaten with a simple yogurt and cucumber salad.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: masoor-dal → red lentils (split masoor) — cook for only 8 minutes as they soften faster (texture will be softer and less distinct); dates → dried figs or chopped dried apricots (maintain the sweet-savoury balance); raisin → dried cranberries for a tart note (changes the sweetness profile slightly)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Whole masoor lentils, basmati rice, dates (khajoor), raisins, ghee and all the spices are available in every Indian grocery store. The dum technique is identical to biryani — use a thick-bottomed handi or pressure cooker base (without the lid locked). Khajoor rice has a long tradition in Indian households too, making this very approachable.
